author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
kadamba tarura ḍāla, nāmiyāche bhūme bhāla,
phuṭiyāche phula sāri sāri
parimale bharala, sakala vṛndāvana,
keli kare bhramarā bhramarī

rāi⋅kānu vilāsoi raṅge
kibā rūpa lāvaṇi, vaidagdhakhani dhani,
maṇimaya ābhāraṇa āṅge

rādhāra dakṣiṇa kara, dhari’ priya giridhara,
madhura madhura cali’ jāya
āge pāche sakhī-gaṇa, kare phula variṣaṇa,
kona sakhī cāmara ḍhulāya

parāge dhūsara sthala, candra kare suśītala,
maṇimaya vedīra upare
rāi⋅kānu kara joḍi’, nṛtya kare phiri’ phiri’,
paraśe pulake tanu bhare

tāra madhye hemapīṭha, aṣṭadale veṣṭita,
aṣṭadale pradhānā nāyikā
tā’ra madhye ratnāsane, basi āchena duijane,
śyāma saṅge sundarī rādhikā

orūpa lāvaṇyarāśi, amiya paḍeche khasi’,
hāsya parihāsa⋅sambhāṣaṇe
narottama⋅dāsa kaya, nityalīlā sukhamaya,
sadāi sphuruka mora mane

mṛgamada⋅candana, kare kari’ sakhī-gaṇa,
barikhaye phula gandharāje
śramajala bindu bindu, śobhā kare mukha⋅indu,
adhare muralī kibā bāje

hāsya⋅vilāsa⋅rasa, sarala madhura bhāṣa,
narottama⋅manoratha bharu
duhu̐ka vicitra veśa, kusume racita keśa,
locana⋅mohana līlā karu
en_translation:
The branches of the kadamba tree have bent beautifully to the ground, and flowers have blossomed in rows upon rows. The entire Vṛndāvana is filled with fragrance, while bees playfully hum.

Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa enjoy playfully with great joy. Oh, what beauty and charm! A treasure-mine of cleverness, Their bodies are adorned with jeweled ornaments.

Holding Rādhā's right hand, her dear Giridhara walks very sweetly. Before and behind Them, the companions shower flowers, while some fan Them with whisks.

The ground is dusty with pollen, and the moon makes it very cool; upon a jeweled platform, Rādhā and Kṛṣṇa dance with joined hands, turning around and around, Their bodies filled with ecstatic thrills at each other's touch.

In its midst is a golden seat surrounded by eight petals; among them are the principal companions. Upon a jeweled throne, both of Them are seated—beautiful Rādhikā with her dark Lord.

From that abundance of beauty and charm, nectar overflows in Their laughter, jokes, and conversations. Narottama dāsa says: the eternal pastimes are full of joy; may they always manifest in my mind.

Holding musk and sandalwood paste in their hands, the companions shower Them with fragrant flowers. Drops of perspiration adorn Their moon-like faces. Oh, how beautifully the flute plays on His lips!

The mellow of playful laughter and simple sweet speech—may Narottama's heart's desires be fulfilled. In Their wonderful dresses, with Their hair adorned with flowers, They perform eye-enchanting pastimes.
